content.full_text:
California
Fair POlitical
Practices Cornmission
January 10, l-9 91
Eleanor KoppeI
P. O. Box 4055
Lancaster, cA 93539
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our Fi.l,e No. A-9 0-736
Dear l,ls. Koppel:
You have requested advice on behalf of Martin Koppe1
concerning the campaign provisions of the political 
Reform Act
( the "lqgii 1 . 1/
OUESTION
Is Mr. Koppel required to file a candidate intention and
campaign bank account notice (Forms 501 and 5o2) in connection
with a payment from personal funds to pubtish an informational
article in a local advertising publication?
CONCLUSION
Under the circumstances presented in your letter, the Forms
501 and 502 are not required in connection with this expenditure.
FACTS
Hartin xoppel is a mernber of the board of directors of the
Quartz Hill Water District. 
He has never received contributions,
does not intend to receive contributions from others for this
office, and has no campaign bank account or controlled committee.
In addition, he currently does not plan to run for reelection in
November 19 91.
Relyj.ng on telephone advice provided to you on November 16,
1990, by Wayne Imberi of the Comrnission staff, Mr. Koppel paid
from personal funds $109.01 to place an article in a local
advertising publication advising citizens of the district 
that a
t/ 
covernment Code Sections 81ooo-91015. AII statutory
references are to the covernment Code unless otherwise indicated.
Comnission regulations appear at 2 california code of Regulations
Section l-8000, et seq. AIl references to regulations are to Title
2, Divj.sj.on 6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Strccr, Suitt' Strt) a P.O. Rox 8O7 o Sacrirmenro C..\, 95g04-Ottr)7 a (91(r) l2l-56(rtr


Eleanor KoppeI
Page 2
proposed rate hike \ras scheduled for discussion at the December 6,
1990 ueeting of the District's 
board of directors. 
The article
also outlined Mr. Koppel,s objections to the rate increase and
encouraged interested persons to lrrite to him or to attend the
neeting.
You had asked whether such activj.ty would require Mr. Koppel
to fite a candidate intention statement (Forn 5ol-), open a
campaign bank account to receive his personal funds and rnake the
expenditure, and file a canpaign bank account notice (Forn 502).
Iilr. Ixoberi informed you that the Forms 50L and 502 and
establishnent of a carnpaign bank account would not be required.
Your letters, dated November 20, 1990, and December 18, 1990,
correctly summarize Mr. Imberi's advice concerning the filing 
of
Forns 501 and 502. However, your letters also state your
understanding that the expenditure "is not covered under canpaign
statutes,tr which is incorrect and is discussed further belohl.
ANALYSIS
Sections 85200 and 85201 were added to the Act by passage of
Proposition 73 in the June 1988 prirnary election. z/ Section 85200
states :
Prior to the solicitation or receipt of any contribution
or loan, an individual vho intends to be a candj.date for an
elective office shaIl file vith the commission a statement
signed under penalty of perjury of intention to be a
candidate for a specific office.
Section 85201 provides:
(a) Upon the filing of the statement of intention
pursuant to Section 85200, the indj.viduaL shall, establish one
campaiqn contribution account at an office of a financial
i.nstitution located in the state.
(b) Upon the establishment of an account, the name of
the financial institution, 
the specific location, and the
account number shall be filed with the commission within 24
hours .(c) A11 contributions or loans nade to the candidate,
to a person on behalf of the candidate, or to the candidate,s
controlled comrnittee shall be deposited in the account.
't 
On September 25, 2990, the United States District Court
Eastern Di;trict 
of California, in the case of service rnplJiees
fnternational Union. et a1. v. Fair PoIitical Practices Commission
(Case No. CIVS 89-0433 LKK-JFM), ruled that portions of
Proposition 73 were unconstitutional. 
However, the portions of
Proposition 73 discussed in this letter were unaffected by this
decis ion .


Eleanor KoppeI
Page 3
(d) Any personaL funds which lrill be utilized to
pronote the election of the candidate shall be deposited in
the account prior to expenditure.
(e) AII carnpaign expenditures shall be made from the
account .
Prior to soliciting or receivj,ng any contribution or 1oan,
even for the purpose of paying officeholder expenses, Mr. Koppel
must file a candidate intention statement (Forn 5o1). The
definition of rrcontribution, t' as provided in Section 82015 of the
Act, includes rrthe candidate,s own noney or property used on
behalf of his or her candidacy.'r Because Mr. Koppel,s personal
funds were not used on behalf of his candidacy, the payment does
not appear to come within the Act,s definition of I'contribution. tl
Therefore, Ur. Koppel is not required to file a candidate
intention (Forn 501) in connection with the pa)rment.
Section 85202 (d) requires that personal funds of a candidate
or of f icehol-der be deposited into a campaign bank account before
being spent only if the funds 'lwi1l be utilized to prornote the
election of the candidate.'r In addition, Regulation 18525 (copy
enclosed) outlines those expenditures a candidate or officeholder
might nake that would be considered trcampaign relatedrt and must be
made from a specified canpaign bank account. The article
published by Mr. Koppe] does not promote his candidacy and is not
covered under Regulation 18525. Therefore, Mr. Koppel rras not
required to open a campaign bank account in order to nake this
expenditure or to file Form 5o2.
However, the pa)rment is a reportable rtexpendituret' under the
Act and must be counted for purposes of filing 
serni-annual
canpaign reports. 
The term rrexpenditurett is defined in Section
A2O25 of the Act and by Regulation 18225 to nean:
...any monetary or nonmonetary paynent nade for pol,itical
purposes. A payment is made for political purposes if it
is:...
(2) Made by:
(A) A candidate, unless it is clear from
surrounding circurnstances that the pa)rment was rnade for
personal purposes unrelated to his or her candidacy or
status as an office holder....
Regulation L8225 (a) (2) .
If Mr. Koppelrs total receipts or total expenditures for
1990, including the expenditure for the article, 
reached g1,0OO or
more, he is required to disclose those activities 
on the Candidate
and Officeholder Campaign Statenent--Long Form (Forn 490, copy
enclosed) no later than January 31, L991. If his total recej.pts
and expenditures were less than $1,OOO for aII of 1990, he Inay use
the short form carnpaign statement (Form 470, copy enclosed) . If


Eleanor Koppel
Page 4
!1r. Koppel has already filed Form 47o tor 1990, he is not required
to file any other campaign statements for the year as long as
total receipts and totat expenditures remained less than $1,0o0
for the entire year. (Sections a42OO, et seo.; Regulation 18406.)
If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to
contact ne at (916) 322-5662.
Sincerely,
scott Hallabrin
Acting ceneral counsel

By: Carla Wardlow
Assistant Pivision chief
Technical Assistance and
Analysis Division
Encl-osures

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Is Mr. Koppel required to file a candidate intention and
campaign bank account notice (Forms 501 and 5o2) in connection
with a payment from personal funds to pubtish an informational
article in a local advertising publication?

CONCLUSION: Under the circumstances presented in your letter, the Forms
501 and 502 are not required in connection with this expenditure.
